compiled_state.title: Add interactivity question and non-interactive deferral to debug-everything
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Add an interactivity question at debug-everything session start and non-interactive deferral behavior for all user-input-requiring gates.

**File**: `plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md`

**Implementation**:
1. **Add interactivity question** after session lock (Phase 1 Step 1): Ask user "Is this an interactive session? (yes/no)" via AskUserQuestion. Store the response as an interactivity flag for the session.
2. **Non-interactive deferral logic**: When non-interactive is selected, at any point where the skill would normally block for user input:
   - Leave the bug open (do not attempt the fix requiring user input)
   - Add classification comment: `ticket comment <id> "DEFERRED: Non-interactive session. Gate: <gate-name>. Reason: <why user input needed>"`
   - Continue to next bug or next phase
3. **Gate Phase 2.6 safeguard approval**: When non-interactive, auto-defer all safeguard bugs (skip the user approval presentation). Log: "Non-interactive: deferring N safeguard bugs."
4. **Gate COMPLEX_ESCALATION (Phase 6 Step 3a / Phase 9)**: When non-interactive, log the COMPLEX_ESCALATION summary as a ticket comment instead of presenting to user for decision. Defer the bug.
5. **Propagate to fix-bug invocations**: When invoking fix-bug (both in Bug-Fix Mode and Phase 5 sub-agent dispatch), include the interactivity flag in the context: `Interactivity: non-interactive — defer items requiring user input`
6. **Validation loop integration** (from story f352-70ad): When non-interactive, bugs discovered during validation loop are auto-fixed without user prompting up to max iterations.

**TDD**: After this change, Task 49d7-a0f3's 6 tests turn GREEN.
**Depends on**: Task 49d7-a0f3

### Files to modify
- EDIT: `plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Interactivity question documented at session start
  Verify: grep -qi 'interactive.*session\|session.*interactive' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md
- [ ] Non-interactive deferral documented
  Verify: grep -qi 'non.interactive.*defer\|defer.*non.interactive\|DEFERRED.*non.interactive' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md
- [ ] Phase 2.6 safeguard approval gated by interactivity
  Verify: python3 -c "
import re, sys
c = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
m = re.search(r'Phase 2\.6.*?(?=\n## Phase [3-9])', c, re.DOTALL)
assert m, 'Phase 2.6 not found'
s = m.group(0)
assert 'non-interactive' in s.lower() or 'interactivity' in s.lower(), 'no interactivity gate in Phase 2.6'
" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md
- [ ] Existing delegation test still passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-debug-everything-fix-bug-delegation.sh
- [ ] Existing escalation test still passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-debug-everything-escalation.sh

comments.body: AC amendment: MISSING GATES — Also gate these interaction points in non-interactive mode: Phase 6 Step 1a (file-overlap escalation), Phase 6 Step 1b (oscillation-guard escalation), Phase 9 Step 4 (escalated bug presentation). Total 5 interaction points to gate, not just 2.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: AC amendment: DEFERRED COMMENT FORMAT — Use machine-parseable format: 'INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: <gate_name> | <context_summary>' where gate_name is one of: safeguard_approval, complex_escalation, file_overlap, oscillation_guard, bug_accountability. Context includes enough state to resume in next interactive session.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: AC amendment: RESUME BEHAVIOR — Document as known limitation: Phase 1 resume logic only scans CHECKPOINT lines, not INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED. Next interactive session must manually check open bugs for DEFERRED comments. Creating a follow-up ticket for automated resume is acceptable.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: AC amendment: FIX-BUG PROPAGATION — Clarify: all deferral decisions are made at the debug-everything orchestrator level. fix-bug sub-agents do NOT need to honor the interactivity flag themselves — the orchestrator intercepts COMPLEX_ESCALATION reports and defers them. Document this in the interactivity section.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: AC amendment: DEFAULT BEHAVIOR — If interactivity question is unanswered (timeout or skip), default to interactive mode (preserving current behavior). Document explicitly.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Interactivity question + non-interactive deferral for all 5 interaction gates.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
